---
name: codebase-reading
description: Use when reading, explaining, or summarizing existing code, services, modules, routes, handlers, queries, or flows.
---

# Codebase Reading

## When To Use

Use when the user asks what code does, where logic lives, or how a flow works.

## Rules

- Explain the existing code before suggesting improvements.
- Trace from entry point to downstream dependencies.
- Mention file paths, symbols, data models, and side effects.
- Separate facts from assumptions.
- Call out missing tests, docs, and unknown business rules.

## Output

- Entry point
- Flow
- Business logic
- Data contracts
- Dependencies
- Error handling
- Security/privacy
- Tests/docs
